claim: Cantonese finite clauses permit null subjects and null objects
exact_support: Article p.195 states that Cantonese allows both null subjects and null objects in finite clauses
limitations: Detailed inspected discussion concentrates on null objects rather than a full null-subject distribution
parser_implication: Keep subject and object omission as separate research subfamilies

claim: A specific null object must be previously mentioned or contextually recoverable
exact_support: Example (5): 件衫好靚喎 / 我鍾意呀; discussion says the object referent is supplied by the preceding discourse
limitations: Paper adopts a null-topic analysis and is principally a bilingual-child transfer study
parser_implication: Use the surface recoverability condition without adopting one silent-topic ontology

claim: Overt topicalization and null-object interpretation are related
exact_support: Examples (5)-(6) compare an understood object with an overt blouse topic
limitations: Does not prove that every null object has one derivation or that all overt topics license omission
parser_implication: Represent overt-topic antecedence as one licensed path, not the universal rule

claim: Cantonese permits anaphoric non-specific object drop
limitations: Theoretical derivation is author-specific and examples are largely constructed
parser_implication: Do not restrict omitted objects to definite coreference

claim: Non-specific object drop divides into existential, generic, and attributive subtypes
limitations: Subtype classification needs corpus and broader replication
parser_implication: Preserve reference type instead of one null-object flag

claim: One surface null object may be ambiguous between specific and non-specific readings
exact_support: Examples (6)-(7) explicitly list same-individual and different-individual readings
limitations: Lexical and contextual factors controlling the choice are not exhaustively tested
parser_implication: Allow multiple readings and context-required status

claim: The classification examples were acceptable to 20 Hong Kong-born Cantonese speakers aged 15-45
exact_support: Footnote 3 reports unanimous acceptability for section 3.1 examples
limitations: Small convenience judgment check; no item-level data, controls, or regional comparison
parser_implication: Treat as attestation support, not productivity or promotion evidence

claim: Predicate semantics affects interpretation of an omitted object
exact_support: Discussion of attributive/intensional object drop notes the role of attitude verb 要/want
parser_implication: Future tests must stratify verbs and complement types

claim: NP-ellipsis with a null determiner is one proposed derivation
limitations: Competing null-topic, argument-ellipsis, and bare-NP analyses remain; source itself notes DP controversy
parser_implication: Do not hard-code the theoretical derivation as parser ontology

claim: Cantonese VP ellipsis is selectively licensed by particular heads
exact_support: Examples (3), (5)-(13) and Tables 1-2 distinguish licensing modals/perfectives from non-licensing progressive/aspectual heads
limitations: Theory-specific phase analysis; examples include movement/postposing as well as ellipsis
parser_implication: Separate VP/complement ellipsis from nominal argument drop

claim: Progressive 喺度 and some aspectual verbs do not license VP ellipsis
limitations: Spatial 喺度 remains possible and pragmatically distinct
parser_implication: Do not infer an elided VP from every short aspectual expression

claim: Chinese-type topic drop should be distinguished from other pro-drop phenomena
exact_support: Abstract and article distinguish null topic, pro drop, CP-edge locality, and root-clause effects
limitations: Evidence is not specifically a Cantonese distribution study
parser_implication: Use only as a boundary question; require Cantonese confirmation

claim: Runtime already links an overt topic to a missing object in a narrow TopicChain path
exact_support: Probe 呢本書，我睇完喇 returns licensed_overt_topic_chain and linked_null_object_count 1
limitations: Internal behavior is not linguistic evidence and does not cover implicit discourse topics
parser_implication: Preserve narrow implementation fact; re-audit before any code change
